title: Decision Quality Wiring, Cognitive Flexibility & Journal Reflection
rationale: Phase 16 added readability metrics, linguistic style drift, reliability scoring, graph topology, and negation-aware biases. Web research + codebase gap analysis reveals 3 critical gaps: (1) DecisionQualityScorer (US-321/US-328) computes an 8-dimension quality score but it is NEVER blended into the final readiness_score — it only appears in the signal for display. This is a Phase 14 feature that was never completed. (2) DecisionQualityScorer uses pure keyword matching (hardcoded word lists) with no semantic understanding — 'I carefully studied the chart' scores 0.0 on information_adequacy because 'studied' isn't in the keyword list. (3) No cognitive flexibility measurement — research (Frontiers Neuroscience 2024, Dajani & Uddin) shows belief-updating ability is one of the strongest predictors of trading success, distinct from but complementary to confirmation bias detection. Additionally: (4) AdaptiveWeightManager (US-302) requires 30+ samples to activate but most sessions never accumulate enough — it effectively never fires. (5) No journal reflection quality scoring — Harvard journaling research shows structured reflective writing quality predicts 22.8% performance improvement, and automated reflection assessment is feasible via causal language density + depth classification (British Journal Ed Tech 2025). Phase 17 focuses on completing the decision quality pipeline, measuring cognitive flexibility, and scoring journal reflection depth.

stories.title: Wire decision quality score into readiness composite
stories.description: DecisionQualityScorer (US-321) computes an 8-dimension composite but the value is ONLY stored in ReadinessSignal.decision_quality_score for display — it never influences the actual readiness_score that Buddy reads. This means excellent decision process doesn't raise readiness and terrible decision process doesn't lower it. Wire decision_quality_score into the readiness computation at 7% weight (scale recovery from 8% to 6%, reduce v1_raw graph blend from 10% to 9% to make room). Apply as a post-V1/V2 blend like recovery_score. Also add a warning when decision quality < 30 but readiness > 70: 'high readiness with poor decision process — verify before trading'.
acceptance: After decision_quality_score_val is computed (line ~1365), blend into readiness_score: readiness_score = 0.93 * readiness_score + 0.07 * decision_quality_score_val
Clamp result to [0, 100]
Recovery weight reduced from 8% to 6%: readiness_score = 0.94 * readiness_score + 0.06 * (recovery_score * 100)
Graph blend reduced from 10% to 9%: v1_raw = 0.91 * v1_raw + 0.09 * graph_context_score
Warning logged when decision_quality_score > 0 AND < 30 AND readiness_score > 70
State snapshot includes 'decision_quality_blended': True

At least 8 tests: DQ blend raises readiness when quality high, DQ blend lowers readiness when quality low, DQ=0 (no scorer) has no effect, DQ warning on high readiness + low quality, recovery weight change doesn't break existing recovery tests, graph weight change verified, DQ + recovery combined blend, clamping at boundaries

stories.title: Semantic decision quality signals replacing keyword matching
stories.description: DecisionQualityScorer (US-321) uses 8 hardcoded keyword lists where 'I carefully studied the chart' scores 0.0 on information_adequacy because 'studied' isn't in the keyword list, and 'I didn't check anything' still matches on 'check'. Replace keyword matching with 3 improvements: (1) Phrase pattern matching — use regex patterns that capture semantic intent ('check|review|analyz|stud|examin|look at|went through' for information gathering). (2) VADER sentiment on dimension-specific text — extract sentences mentioning each dimension, score their sentiment. (3) Negation awareness — reuse the _is_negated() pattern from US-336 to avoid 'I didn't check' scoring as information gathering. Keep existing keyword lists as fallback. Score = max(keyword_score, semantic_score) for backward compatibility.
acceptance: DecisionQualityScorer gains _semantic_score_dimension(text, dimension_name) method

8 regex pattern sets: one per dimension (process_adherence, information_adequacy, metacognitive_awareness, uncertainty_acknowledgment, metacognitive_monitoring, rationale_clarity, emotional_regulation, cognitive_reflection)
Each regex set uses word stems and alternation (e.g., 'check|review|analyz|stud|examin|assess|evaluat|inspect')
Negation filter applied: if sentence containing match has negation within 3 tokens before, exclude it
Final dimension score = max(keyword_based_score, semantic_score) for backward compatibility
VADER applied to rationale_clarity: compound score > 0.3 on rationale sentences boosts clarity by 0.1

At least 8 tests: 'studied the chart' now scores > 0 on info_adequacy, 'didn't check' scores 0, negation works on all 8 dimensions, semantic scores higher than keyword for rich text, keyword fallback works on exact matches, backward compatible on existing test inputs, VADER boost on clear rationale, empty text safe

stories.title: Cognitive flexibility scorer: belief updating and strategy adaptation
stories.description: Cognitive flexibility — the ability to update beliefs when evidence contradicts them — is one of the strongest predictors of trading success (Dajani & Uddin 2015, Frontiers Neuroscience 2024). It's distinct from confirmation bias (which detects rigidity) by measuring the POSITIVE signal: does the trader actively revise their view? Implement CognitiveFlexibilityScorer with 3 metrics: (1) belief_update_language — detects phrases like 'changed my mind', 'I was wrong', 'updated my thesis', 'reconsidered', 'new information changed'. (2) strategy_adaptation_language — 'adjusted my approach', 'switching to', 'modified my plan', 'different strategy'. (3) evidence_acknowledgment — 'despite my expectation', 'contrary to what I thought', 'the data shows otherwise'. Composite flexibility score blended into readiness as a bonus (not penalty): high flexibility adds 3 to 5 points. Low flexibility with high confirmation bias triggers warning.
acceptance: New class CognitiveFlexibilityScorer in src/aura/scoring/cognitive_flexibility.py
score(text) -> CognitiveFlexibilityResult with belief_update (0-1), strategy_adaptation (0-1), evidence_acknowledgment (0-1), composite (0-1)
Keyword/phrase lists for each metric (at least 8 phrases per metric)
Negation-aware: 'I didn't change my mind' does NOT score as flexibility
composite = 0.40 * belief_update + 0.35 * strategy_adaptation + 0.25 * evidence_acknowledgment
ReadinessComputer integrates: if composite > 0.3, apply flexibility_bonus = min(5.0, composite * 7.0)
Warning when flexibility < 0.1 AND confirmation_bias > 0.5: 'rigid thinking detected'
ConversationSignals gains cognitive_flexibility_score: float field (0-1)

At least 8 tests: belief update phrases detected, strategy adaptation detected, evidence acknowledgment detected, negated phrases excluded, composite weighting correct, flexibility bonus applied to readiness, warning on rigidity, empty text returns 0.0

stories.title: Bootstrap adaptive weights from outcome history + lower activation threshold
stories.description: AdaptiveWeightManager (US-302) uses Beta-Binomial conjugate priors to learn per-component importance from trade outcomes. However, is_ready() requires MIN_SAMPLES (30) which most sessions never reach — the adaptive system effectively never activates. Two fixes: (1) Lower MIN_SAMPLES from 30 to 10 to allow earlier learning. (2) Add bootstrap_from_history(outcomes: List[Dict]) method that replays past outcome signals to warm-start the priors. ReadinessComputer calls bootstrap on initialization if outcome_signal.json history is available. Add /weights CLI command showing current component weights (static vs adaptive) with visual bars.
acceptance: AdaptiveWeightManager.MIN_SAMPLES changed from 30 to 10
New method bootstrap_from_history(outcomes: List[Dict]) replays outcome data through update()
Each outcome dict must have 'success' (bool) and 'component_scores' (Dict[str, float])
bootstrap_from_history validates input: skips entries with missing keys, logs count of valid vs skipped
ReadinessComputer.__init__() attempts to bootstrap from bridge outcome_signal.json if it exists
If bootstrap loads >= 10 outcomes, adaptive weights activate immediately
AuraCompanion handles /weights command: displays per-component weight (static and adaptive if active) with visual bars

At least 8 tests: bootstrap with 15 valid outcomes activates adaptive, bootstrap with 5 outcomes stays inactive, bootstrap skips malformed entries, MIN_SAMPLES=10 verified, /weights command output format, weight values sum to ~1.0, integration with compute() after bootstrap, empty history file handled safely

stories.title: Journal reflection quality scoring: depth, causal density, and pre-mortem presence
stories.description: Research (British Journal of Ed Tech 2025, Frontiers Psychology 2021) shows reflective writing quality predicts future decision quality. Implement JournalReflectionScorer with 3 metrics: (1) reflection_depth — classify text into 4 levels: L1=summary only ('took trade, lost'), L2=some reasoning ('thought it would breakout'), L3=causal analysis ('misjudged volatility because...'), L4=meta-reflection ('this pattern shows I overestimate edge in choppy markets'). Use causal language markers ('because', 'caused by', 'led to', 'as a result', 'due to') + meta-markers ('pattern shows', 'I notice', 'this tells me', 'lesson learned') for classification. (2) causal_density — count of explicit cause-effect statements / total sentences. (3) premortem_presence — did trader imagine failure before entering? ('if this fails', 'risk is', 'what could go wrong', 'worst case'). Feed reflection quality into decision_quality_score as a modulator: high reflection boosts DQ by up to 10%.
acceptance: New class JournalReflectionScorer in src/aura/scoring/journal_reflection.py
score(text) -> JournalReflectionResult with depth_level (1-4), causal_density (0-1), premortem_present (bool), reflection_quality (0-1)
Depth classification: L1 if no reasoning markers, L2 if reasoning but no causal, L3 if causal markers present, L4 if meta-reflection markers present
causal_density = causal_statements / max(1, total_sentences)
premortem_present = any premortem phrase found in text
reflection_quality = 0.50 * (depth_level / 4.0) + 0.30 * causal_density + 0.20 * (1.0 if premortem_present else 0.0)
DecisionQualityScorer optionally receives reflection_quality and applies: composite *= (1.0 + 0.10 * reflection_quality) capped at 100

At least 8 tests: L1 text classified correctly, L4 text classified correctly, causal density computed, premortem detected, premortem absent, reflection modulates DQ score, empty text returns L1 with 0.0 quality, integration with decision quality composite

stories.title: Phase 17 integration tests + /flexibility and /journal CLI commands
stories.description: Write integration tests verifying the full Phase 17 pipeline: (1) Decision quality blended into readiness score. (2) Semantic scoring outperforms keyword-only on rich text. (3) Cognitive flexibility bonus applied to readiness. (4) Adaptive weights bootstrap from history. (5) Journal reflection quality modulates decision quality. Plus add /flexibility CLI command showing flexibility scores with belief/strategy/evidence breakdown. Add /journal CLI command showing reflection depth level, causal density, and premortem presence for last 5 messages.
acceptance: At least 3 integration tests using real instances (no mocks on production code)
Test 1: Rich decision text with DQ score > 50 -> readiness increases by 3-5 points vs no DQ blend
Test 2: Text with 'changed my mind' + 'new data shows' -> flexibility > 0.3 -> readiness bonus applied
Test 3: Bootstrap 15 outcomes -> adaptive weights active -> compute() uses learned weights
Test 4: 'I misjudged because...' text -> reflection depth L3 -> DQ gets 10% boost
Test 5: Semantic scoring: 'carefully studied the chart' -> info_adequacy > 0.3 (keyword-only would give 0.0)
AuraCompanion handles /flexibility command: displays composite + 3 sub-scores with interpretation
AuraCompanion handles /journal command: displays depth level, causal density, premortem status for recent messages
All integration tests use NEUTRAL_CIRCADIAN to isolate from time-of-day effects
At least 8 tests total across integration + CLI scenarios
